primary_marker.rationale: Absolute eosinophilia can support clinically significant eosinophilic disease evaluation when persistent or organ-related features are present.

supporting_markers.rationale: Tryptase can help identify a clonal or myeloid-associated eosinophilic process in selected cases.
Inflammatory marker elevation can support a more active systemic disease context when eosinophilia is persistent.

hypotheses.physiological_claim: High absolute eosinophils may reflect a persistent secondary eosinophilic process related to allergic, drug-related, infectious, or autoimmune drivers.
caveats: Secondary causes are more common than primary hypereosinophilic syndromes.

missing_data.policy: Exclude common secondary causes before prioritising clonal or idiopathic eosinophilic syndromes.

contradiction_markers.contradiction_rationale: A clearly normal CRP weakens an active systemic inflammatory secondary eosinophilia interpretation, as significant inflammatory or autoimmune-driven eosinophilia is typically associated with concurrent systemic inflammation.

hypotheses.physiological_claim: Persistent marked absolute eosinophilia may justify evaluation for hypereosinophilic or clonal eosinophilic syndromes when secondary causes are not evident.
caveats: This interpretation should remain cautious until persistence, organ involvement, and secondary-cause exclusion are established.

missing_data.policy: Review persistence, symptoms, organ involvement, and specialist hematologic markers before assigning a rare eosinophilic syndrome frame.

contradiction_markers.contradiction_rationale: Rapid normalization after removal of a secondary trigger weakens a clonal or persistent hypereosinophilic interpretation.

confirmatory_tests.rationale: Persistence-focused eosinophilia workup helps distinguish common secondary causes from rare hypereosinophilic syndromes.

override_rules.description: Escalate when high absolute eosinophils coincide with high tryptase, as this can support a myeloid or clonal eosinophilic context.

sources.paper_title: Approach to the patient with eosinophilia

evidence.physiological_claim: Persistent absolute eosinophilia can justify structured evaluation because the differential includes both common secondary causes and rarer clonal eosinophilic syndromes.
evidence.threshold_notes: Interpretation should emphasize persistence and organ context rather than assign rare syndrome labels from a single isolated result.

narrative.mechanism: Persistent eosinophilia can result from chronic immune stimulation, tissue eosinophilic disease, or rarely clonal hematologic drivers.
narrative.biological_pathway: Sustained eosinophil production and survival across reactive and clonal eosinophilic pathways.
narrative.interpretation: This pattern is compatible with several disease classes and should prompt structured differential review rather than premature labeling.
narrative.implications: The main distinction is between common secondary causes and the smaller subset that needs specialist eosinophilic syndrome assessment.
narrative.supporting_marker_roles: CRP supports systemic inflammatory context, while tryptase can help flag a possible clonal eosinophilic process.
